name: Power Macs 'won't hit 3GHz this year' – analysts
description: Macworld UK news piece (2004-06-11) reporting that Apple's self-imposed one-year 3GHz G5 target will miss its 11 June 2004 deadline, with analysts attributing the delay to industry-wide problems scaling the 90-nanometer manufacturing process. Peter Kastner, EVP and chief research officer at Aberdeen Research Group, quantifies the industry slowdown: Intel managed only 3.2→3.4GHz in the quarter while IBM got an 'A' for cranking the G5 from 2GHz to 2.5GHz — a 25% jump. Kastner predicts the industry will not reach 3GHz for another year.
